'''Constantine Mika''' (born 14 September 1989) is a [[Samoa national rugby league team|Samoa]] international [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sr}} or {{rlp|lf}} for the [[XIII Limouxin]]. He previously played for the [[Newcastle Knights]] in the [[National Rugby League|NRL]], [[Hull Kingston Rovers]] in the [[Super League]], [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], as well as a spell playing [[rugby union]] for [[Provence Rugby]] in the French Pro D2.

==Background==
Mika was born in [[Auckland]], New Zealand.

==Early years==
Mika joined the [[New Zealand Warriors]] from the local [[Auckland Rugby League]] competition where he played for [[Otahuhu Leopards|Otahuhu]] and the [[Tamaki Titans]] in the [[Bartercard Cup]]. He made two appearances in the [[National Youth Competition (rugby league)|Toyota Cup]] for the Junior Warriors, however he could not break into the Warriors first grade team.<ref>{{cite web|url=http://www.nzleague.co.nz/warriors/U20_player_profile.php?letter=M&id=26&name=Mika |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111003225820/http://www.nzleague.co.nz/warriors/U20_player_profile.php?letter=M&id=26&name=Mika%2C |archive-date=3 October 2011 |work=nzleague.co.nz |url-status=dead |title=Mika, Constantine}}</ref>

==Newcastle Knights==
Mika joined the [[Newcastle Knights]] from the 2009 season.<ref name="2009 NRL Player Movements">{{cite web|url=http://www.nrllive.com.au/2009-nrl-player-movements/|title=2009 NRL Player Movements|publisher=NRL Live|date=4 October 2008|accessdate=4 October 2008|url-status=dead|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20081005172545/http://www.nrllive.com.au/2009-nrl-player-movements/|archive-date=5 October 2008}}</ref> He made his first grade début in round 12 on 31 May 2009 against [[Canterbury Bulldogs|Canterbury]]. In June 2010 he quoted ''"Coming over here was the best move I've made for my footy career,"''<ref>{{cite news| url=http://www.smh.com.au/rugby-league/league-news/mika-the-mack-truck-ready-to-dent-more-reputations-20100610-y0lh.html| work=The Sydney Morning Herald| title=Mika the Mack truck ready to dent more reputations| first=Robert| last=Dillon| date=11 June 2010}}</ref>

After the conclusion of the 2011 season, Mika was released by the Newcastle club.<ref>{{cite news|last=Keeble|first= Brett|date=6 September 2011|url= https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/475702/knights-in-no-hurry-to-go-separate-ways/|title=Knights in no hurry to go separate ways – Local News – Sport – Rugby League – Newcastle Herald|newspaper=The Herald|access-date=4 October 2011}}</ref>

==Hull Kingston Rovers==
Mika signed with Rovers on 26 October 2011. It was a two-year deal to join, also departing from the Knights, the new Rovers head coach, Craig Sandercock and fullback [[Shannon McDonnell (rugby league)|Shannon McDonnell]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.newcastleherald.com.au/story/477636/knights-sign-adam-cuthbertson/|title=Knights sign Adam Cuthbertson|first=Robert|last=Dillon|date=26 October 2011|newspaper=The Herald|access-date=19 February 2017|archive-date=27 October 2011|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20111027070426/http://www.theherald.com.au/news/local/sport/rugby-league/knights-sign-adam-cuthbertson/2337043.aspx|url-status=live}}</ref>

==Return to Australia==
After completing a stint in the [[Super League]], Mika returned to Australia to compete in the [[Queensland Cup]] and debuted for the [[Redcliffe Dolphins]] in 2014. The following year, Mika signed with the Wave Tigers of the Bundaberg Rugby League competition.<ref>{{cite news |title=Mika ready to make an impact |url=http://www.news-mail.com.au/news/mika-ready-to-make-an-impact/2549971/ |work=couriermail |date=20 February 2015 }}</ref>

==France==
Mika changed codes to [[rugby union]] and signed for newly promoted [[Provence Rugby]] of the Pro D2 in the summer of 2015, to play at centre.

===Toulouse Olympique===
After one season playing union Mika returned to rugby league to join [[Toulouse Olympique]] in 2016.<ref>{{Cite web|url=http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/news/article/480/10300110/toulouse-strengthen-with-signing-of-con-mika|title=Toulouse swoop for Mika|accessdate=14 July 2021|archive-date=1 January 2017|archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/20170101172122/http://www.sportinglife.com/rugby-league/news/article/480/10300110/toulouse-strengthen-with-signing-of-con-mika|url-status=dead}}</ref>
 
After four seasons with Toulouse Mike left to join another French team.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=30 Jul 2020|accessdate=11 Jan 2021|title=Constantine Mika quits Toulouse after four years|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/constantine-mika-quits-toulouse-after-four-years/}}</ref>

===Villeneuve XIII RLLG===
Mika signed for [[Villeneuve XIII RLLG]] in the [[Elite One Championship]] in July 2020 and played the rest of the French domestic season with the club<ref>{{cite web|website=Trieze Mondial.fr|date=30 Jul 2020|accessdate=11 Jan 2021|title=Constantine Mika signe à Villeneuve XIII|url=https://treizemondial.fr/constantine-mika-signe-a-villeneuve-xiii/}}</ref>

===Brooklyn Kings===
In April 2021 Mika signed for [[Brooklyn Kings RLFC]] in the [[North American Rugby League]] (NARL).<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=16 Apr 2021|accessdate=17 Apr 2021|title=Brooklyn Kings sign former Samoa international Constantine Mika|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/brooklyn-kings-sign-former-samoa-international-constantine-mika/}}</ref> When the first season of the NARL was cancelled Mika became a free agent.<ref name="LRL140721"/>

===Keighley Cougars===
Mika's signing for English side, Keighley Cougars, for the remainder of the 2021 season was announced on 14 July 2021.<ref name="LRL140721">{{cite news |last1=Darbyshire |first1=Drew |title=Constantine Mika joins Keighley |url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/constantine-mika-joins-keighley/ |access-date=14 July 2021 |work=LoveRugbyLeague |date=14 July 2021}}</ref>

===Limoux Grizzlies===
On 5 August 2022, it was announced that Mika had signed for [[Limoux Grizzlies]] for the 2022-23 season.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|title=Constantine Mika returns to French Elite One|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/constantine-mika-returns-to-french-elite-one/ |accessdate=10 Oct 2022|date=5 Aug 2022}}</ref>

===Nyngan Tigers===
On 16 June 2023, Mika signed for the [[Nyngan Tigers]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.dailyliberal.com.au/story/8236391/former-nrl-player-and-samoan-representative-to-bolster-the-tigers-forward-pack/|title=Peter McDonald Premiership: Nyngan have signed Constantine Mika for 2023|website=www.dailyliberal.com.au|date=16 June 2023 }}</ref>
